notable.headline: DoD Cyber Budget Surge: $2.1B in New Obligations This Quarter
notable.summary: The Department of Defense has accelerated cyber spending by 34% QoQ, with CYBERCOM and DISA leading procurement. Three major recompetes worth $890M are hitting the street in the next 60 days. If you hold incumbent positions in network defense or zero-trust architecture, now is the time to prep your capture teams.

doge_tracker.headline: DOGE-driven workforce cuts at USAID and OPM are creating downstream contract disruptions -- 14 active contracts worth $2.3B have lost their COR or program office oversight this month alone.

agencies_affected.impact: 127 active contracts ($4.1B) now have no assigned Contracting Officer's Representative. Stop-work orders issued on 23 task orders. Primes are burning fee on idle staff.
agencies_affected.status: Injunction filed -- temporary restraining order in effect through April 2, but agency is slow-walking re-staffing. Don't count on resumption.

agencies_affected.impact: HR IT modernization contracts ($380M across 6 vehicles) are in limbo. OPM issued informal 'continue performance' guidance but no funded modifications. Contractors billing at risk.
agencies_affected.status: Congressional review period -- expect 90-day delay minimum. If you're subbing on OPM work, get your prime's position in writing NOW.

agencies_affected.impact: FSA loan servicing contracts ($1.8B) are being reviewed for early termination. TIVAS contract holders (Maximus, Aidvantage) face T4C risk. Student loan tech modernization RFPs pulled from forecast.
agencies_affected.status: Legislation pending. No imminent action but pipeline is frozen. BD teams should pause capture spend on ED opportunities until Q3 FY26 clarity.

agencies_affected.impact: Superfund remediation contracts and environmental monitoring task orders likely to shrink. Current ceiling on EPA OLEM IDIQ ($420M) may not be fully obligated.
agencies_affected.status: Budget proposal stage -- Congress will negotiate, but signal is clear: environmental services declining 2+ years out.

agencies_affected.action: GROWING -- absorbing mission from other agencies
agencies_affected.impact: DISA picking up shared services IT work previously spread across civilian agencies. DES ceiling increase this week ($450M) is directly tied to this consolidation. Net positive for DISA-focused contractors.
agencies_affected.status: Active growth. Position here if you're losing civilian agency work.

court_rulings.case: National Council of Nonprofits v. OMB
court_rulings.ruling: D.C. District Court extended TRO on USAID contract terminations through April 2, 2026
court_rulings.practical_impact: Contracts are technically still active but CORs are gone and no new task orders are being issued. 'Zombie contracts' -- alive on paper, dead in practice. Don't staff up expecting resumption.
If court rules DOGE advisory committee subject to FACA, all recommendations become advisory-only and agency heads must independently justify cuts. Would slow but not stop the train.

contractor_impact.status: Announced 40% workforce reduction. Teaming partners should assess exposure immediately.
contractor_impact.signal: If you're subbing to Chemonics on anything, have a backup plan by end of month.

contractor_impact.status: Pivoting to State Department and MCC work. May be open to JV arrangements they'd have rejected 6 months ago.
contractor_impact.signal: Opportunity to partner with a firm that has deep international development past performance at a discount.

contractor_impact.status: ED work at risk but HHS and VA portfolios stable. Stock down 18% creates M&A opportunity for mid-tiers looking to acquire federal BPO capabilities.
contractor_impact.signal: Maximus may divest smaller contract vehicles to shore up balance sheet. Watch for novation opportunities.

doge_tracker.outlook: The DOGE disruption is creating a two-speed federal market: DoD and IC are accelerating spend (especially cyber and AI), while civilian agencies face 12-18 months of uncertainty. Smart firms are repositioning BD teams from civilian to defense. The biggest hidden risk is COR attrition -- even contracts that survive cuts may suffer from inadequate government oversight, leading to performance disputes and withheld fees. Build relationships with remaining government staff now; they're overwhelmed and will remember who made their lives easier.

recompete_alerts.title: Recompete Alerts
recompete_alerts.subtitle: Contracts expiring within 180 days -- protect your revenue or start your capture

items.notable_detail: Scope expanding to include zero-trust implementation. Estimated new ceiling $650M. DISA is consolidating 3 legacy support contracts into this single vehicle.
winnability_factors: Leidos has held this since 2018 -- 8 years of incumbency creates some fatigue, but they've performed well (CPARS: Exceptional/Very Good)
Zero-trust expansion opens a lane for firms with strong Zscaler/Palo Alto Prisma implementation credentials
DISA wants contractor consolidation -- firms that can show they'll reduce total vendor count will score higher on management approach
Price competition will be fierce. Leidos will sharpen pencils to defend. Your price-to-win needs to be 8-12% below current run rate
Key discriminator: automated ATO pipeline. DISA's new CIO is obsessed with speeding up authority-to-operate timelines
items.protest_history: EITSS-II was protested by SAIC in 2018 (denied). No sustained protests on this line. DISA has clean procurement record on this vehicle.
who_should_pursue: Large businesses with DISA past performance and existing zero-trust implementations at scale (10K+ endpoints)
Mid-tier firms should team -- lead with zero-trust niche expertise and backfill traditional IT support with proven subs
If you don't already have a relationship with DISA J6, you're too late for prime. Position as sub to Leidos, SAIC, or Peraton.

items.notable_detail: CYBERCOM restructuring into 3 task areas: (1) Defensive Cyber Operations, (2) Threat Intelligence Fusion, (3) Hunt Forward support. Multiple awards likely -- one per task area. This is a MAJOR shaping opportunity.
winnability_factors: Multiple-award structure means you don't have to beat BAH head-to-head -- you can win a task area where they're weakest
Task Area 2 (Threat Intelligence Fusion) is wide open -- BAH's strength is ops, not intel. CrowdStrike, Mandiant/Google, and Recorded Future subs are gold here
Task Area 3 (Hunt Forward) requires deployed experience. Only 4-5 firms in the country have credible Hunt Forward past performance
CYBERCOM is actively seeking new entrants. SIGINT from industry day: 'We don't want a single throat to choke anymore'
Sources Sought responses due April 10 -- if you haven't started your response, drop everything and write it this week
items.protest_history: No protests on current contract. However, CYBERCOM's last 3 major awards have ALL been protested. Budget a 90-day protest delay into your staffing plan.
who_should_pursue: Task Area 1: Large cyber firms with SOC/NOC operations at scale -- SAIC, Leidos, ManTech, Peraton
Task Area 2: Threat intel specialists -- Booz (incumbent advantage), but also CACI, ECS, and commercial threat intel firms with federal arms
Task Area 3: Only pursue if you have actual Hunt Forward or CPT deployment experience. CYBERCOM will verify. Don't waste your B&P.
Small businesses: CYBERCOM is considering an SDVOSB set-aside for a separate Hunt Forward support TO. Watch SAM.gov for NAICS 541519 from CYBERCOM.

items.notable_detail: MHS GENESIS rollout delays may extend incumbent PoP by 6-12 months via bridge contract. BUT -- the recompete will still happen. DHA is under Congressional pressure to compete this. CMMC Level 2 will be a go/no-go requirement.
winnability_factors: Small business set-aside eliminates the primes -- CACI will have to compete through an SB affiliate or lose this
CACI's SB affiliate (Six3 Systems) is the likely incumbent vehicle, but Six3 has grown past SB size standards for 541511 ($34M). Watch for a size protest opportunity.
MHS GENESIS experience is the #1 discriminator. If you've done Cerner/Oracle Health implementations at military treatment facilities, you're in the running
DHA is frustrated with timeline slippage -- position your proposal around schedule discipline and earned value management rigor
CMMC Level 2 certification is a hard gate. Start your assessment NOW if you don't have it. C3PAO wait times are 4-6 months.
items.protest_history: Size protest filed on CACI's SB status during initial award (2021). Dismissed on procedural grounds but the underlying size question remains live.
who_should_pursue: Small businesses with NAICS 541511 under $34M revenue AND Oracle Health/Cerner implementation experience
SDVOSB firms with health IT credentials -- DHA may add an SDVOSB evaluation preference
8(a) firms in the health IT space should contact DHA's OSBP about a potential sole-source bridge if they have relevant past performance
If you're a large business: identify your SB teaming partner NOW and start the mentor-protege paperwork

items.notable_detail: DLA evaluating move to commercial SaaS. The real story: DLA's Deputy Director of Logistics Operations told the NDIA conference last month that 'we're paying custom platform prices for what should be a commercial capability.' Translation: Palantir's pricing model is on the chopping block.
winnability_factors: DLA is signaling they want a commercial solution, not a bespoke platform. This favors Snowflake/Databricks-based approaches over Palantir's Foundry
Palantir's lock-in is real but not insurmountable -- DLA has been quietly building data exports into their contract mods for the past year. They're planning the exit.
Price will be the deciding factor. Palantir's per-seat licensing is 3-4x commercial rates. Come in with a consumption-based model.
The twist: DLA also wants predictive logistics (AI/ML for demand forecasting). Pure SaaS firms need an AI story.
Palantir will protest if they lose. Budget 120 days and have a bridge plan ready.
items.protest_history: Palantir has protested 6 federal contracts in the last 3 years (won 2, denied 4). They WILL protest. Factor this into your timeline.
who_should_pursue: Cloud-native analytics firms (Databricks, Snowflake partners) with FedRAMP High authorization
Mid-tier firms like ECS, Unison, or Definitive Logic that can integrate commercial platforms with DLA's legacy ERP (SAP-based)
Palantir competitors with defense logistics past performance -- C3.ai, Dataiku with federal arms
SIs with strong DLA relationships: Accenture Federal, Deloitte, IBM. They can wrap commercial tech in a managed service.

new_awards.title: New Contract Awards
new_awards.subtitle: Awarded this week -- competitive intelligence for your pipeline

items.notable_detail: Largest C3 integration award this fiscal year. GDIT beat Leidos on technical approach score despite Leidos being 11% lower on price.
items.why_they_won: GDIT's winning move was their 'digital twin' integration testing environment -- they proposed a virtual JADC2 testbed that lets the Army validate cross-domain data flows without live exercises. This cut the estimated T&E timeline by 14 months. They also brought Anduril as a key sub for mesh networking, which signaled commercial tech integration the Army wanted to see. Leidos lost on innovation, not price.
items.what_losers_should_do: Leidos should protest on the best-value tradeoff rationale -- 11% price advantage dismissed is aggressive and GAO may take a hard look. L3Harris should pivot to the sensor-to-shooter sub-tier; GDIT will need RF expertise they don't have in-house. Northrop should focus on the Space-based JADC2 node that PEO Space is expected to solicit in Q4 FY26.
items.market_signal: The Army is willing to pay more for speed and innovation in C3. Traditional 'safe' proposals from incumbent primes are losing to firms that bring commercial tech partners and accelerated delivery models. If you're still proposing waterfall integration approaches for C2 programs, you're going to keep losing.

items.notable_detail: VA's largest single cloud contract. Multi-cloud strategy (AWS + Azure). Accenture's proposal included a FinOps practice that projected $47M in cloud cost optimization over the PoP.
items.why_they_won: Accenture Federal won on two fronts: (1) their embedded FinOps team that guaranteed measurable cloud cost reduction -- VA has been burned by cloud migrations that doubled their hosting costs, and (2) a multi-cloud architecture that avoids vendor lock-in (VA's CTO publicly stated this was a priority after the C2E sole-source controversy). Deloitte's proposal was AWS-only. IBM pushed hybrid cloud with on-prem Z-series -- the VA didn't want more hardware.
items.what_losers_should_do: Deloitte should target the VA's application modernization layer -- CMMS is infrastructure, but the 200+ legacy VistA applications still need to be refactored. That's a separate $500M+ opportunity in FY27. IBM Federal should pivot to the VA's AI/ML initiative for claims processing where their Watson Health lineage still carries weight.
items.market_signal: FinOps is no longer a nice-to-have -- it's becoming an evaluation factor. Agencies that got burned by 'lift and shift' cloud migrations in 2022-2024 are now demanding cost governance as a core deliverable. If your cloud practice doesn't include a FinOps capability with certified practitioners, build it before your next proposal.

items.notable_detail: First major NGA award to a pure-play AI data company. Scale AI's federal division (Scale for Government) has grown from 12 to 180 people in 18 months.
items.why_they_won: Scale AI won because NGA realized their bottleneck isn't algorithms -- it's labeled training data at scale. Scale's existing commercial relationships with Meta and OpenAI gave them a data annotation workforce (100K+ contractors globally) that no defense prime could match. They also proposed a 'data flywheel' where model performance improvements automatically generate better training labels for the next iteration. Palantir proposed Foundry-based labeling but couldn't match throughput. Anthropic's proposal was technically strong but they lacked the cleared facility infrastructure.
items.what_losers_should_do: Palantir should focus on the downstream deployment layer -- Scale curates the data, but someone needs to deploy the trained models into NGA's classified workflows. That integration work is Palantir's sweet spot. Anthropic should invest in SCIF infrastructure and cleared personnel if they're serious about IC work; their technical approach scored highest but facility/clearance gaps killed them. Traditional defense primes: this is your warning shot. AI-native firms are taking market share in the IC. Acquire or partner.
items.market_signal: The IC is unbundling the AI value chain. Data curation, model training, model evaluation, and deployment are being procured separately. This creates entry points for specialist firms at each layer. The days of a single prime owning the entire AI pipeline are ending. Position yourself in one layer and own it.

items.notable_detail: Includes classified resilient comms upgrade. Raytheon leveraged commercial LEO experience from their Starshield work with SpaceX.
items.why_they_won: Raytheon's differentiator was hybrid ground architecture -- they proposed commercial cloud-based ground processing (via AWS Ground Station) for unclassified data, with seamless handoff to classified on-prem processing. This cut estimated ground infrastructure costs by 35%. Their SpaceX Starshield collaboration gave them credible commercial LEO integration experience that LockMart and Ball couldn't match. Ball Aerospace was strong on the sensor side but weak on the network resilience requirements.
items.what_losers_should_do: Lockheed Martin should leverage their GPS OCX experience to target the follow-on PNT ground modernization expected in FY27. Ball Aerospace should position as a sub to Raytheon for the sensor calibration task area -- Raytheon will need their expertise. Both should note: SSC is moving toward commercial-first ground architectures. Invest in commercial cloud ground station partnerships.
items.market_signal: Space ground systems are going commercial-hybrid. SSC's acquisition strategy explicitly calls for 'commercial where possible, custom where necessary.' Firms still proposing fully bespoke ground architectures are at a structural disadvantage. The commercial space boom (SpaceX, Amazon Kuiper) is creating a talent and technology pipeline that military-only firms can't access.

option_exercises.title: Option Year Exercises
option_exercises.subtitle: Incumbents extending -- read the signals for recompete timing

items.contract_name: Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification (CMMC) Assessor Support
items.notable_detail: CMMC 2.0 rollout driving 3x assessment volume. ManTech adding 200 assessors. Option exercised 45 days early -- strong signal of government satisfaction.
items.recompete_signal: Two option years remaining means recompete is 2+ years out. No urgency to capture. BUT: the CMMC ecosystem is exploding and ManTech is building an assessor army that will be hard to displace. If you want to compete for CMMC-III, start building your assessor bench NOW -- it takes 12-18 months to certify a CMMC assessor.
who_should_watch: Cybersecurity firms building CMMC assessment practices (Coalfire, Schellman, A-LIGN)
MSPs serving the Defense Industrial Base -- CMMC is creating massive demand for compliance-as-a-service
Any firm planning to bid on DoD contracts after 2027 -- CMMC Level 2 will be table stakes. This is not optional.

items.notable_detail: Exercised despite GAO audit findings on migration timeline. Treasury's CFO office defended CGI's performance in Congressional testimony -- rare and notable public endorsement.
items.recompete_signal: Two option years left, but Treasury has signaled they may not exercise Option Year 5 and instead accelerate recompete to align with the Federal Financial Management Improvement Act milestones. Smart capture managers should start shaping in late FY27, not FY28. The GAO findings create an opening -- Treasury may want fresh eyes even if they're publicly defending CGI.
who_should_watch: SAP and Oracle federal SIs -- Treasury's target state is a unified ERP, and the current system is a patchwork
Mid-tier firms with Treasury/IRS past performance (Guidehouse, Maximus, Unison)
DOGE watchers: Treasury is one of the 'efficiency' targets. If DOGE pushes shared financial services, this contract scope could expand dramatically or be absorbed into a cross-agency vehicle.

items.notable_detail: Final option year. IC already exploring multi-cloud follow-on. Microsoft Azure Government and Google Cloud for Defense both briefed the IC CIO last month. Oracle also positioning via their DoD JWCC win.
items.recompete_signal: THIS IS THE BIG ONE. Final option year means the follow-on RFP should drop within 12 months. The IC's multi-cloud pivot is real -- they've been building abstraction layers on top of C2S for two years specifically to reduce AWS lock-in. The follow-on will almost certainly be multi-award. AWS will retain the largest share but Microsoft and Google will get meaningful work. Estimated follow-on value: $1.5-2B over 5 years.
who_should_watch: Microsoft Azure Government team -- this is their best shot at cracking the IC cloud market. They've invested heavily in air-gapped Azure for TS/SCI.
Google Cloud for Defense -- their Distributed Cloud offering is purpose-built for classified environments. They need this win for credibility.

Cloud SIs (Accenture Federal, Deloitte, SAIC) -- the multi-cloud management layer is where the services money will be. CSPs will fight over IaaS; you should fight over the integration and migration work.
Any firm with IL6/TS cloud experience -- the IC will need migration support to move workloads between CSPs. This is a $200M+ services opportunity adjacent to the main cloud contract.

funding_actions.title: Significant Funding Actions
funding_actions.subtitle: Budget movements, modifications, and ceiling increases -- follow the money

items.notable_detail: Fourth ceiling increase in 18 months. DISA consolidation accelerating faster than planned. SAIC's DES now absorbs IT services from 22 defense agencies. This is becoming the single largest IT services contract in the federal government.
items.intel_note: DES ceiling increases are the clearest signal that DOGE-style consolidation is actually working in DoD. DISA is eating other agencies' IT budgets. If you're not on DES as a sub, you're locked out of a growing $7.25B market. SAIC is actively seeking subs for cloud migration, identity management, and endpoint security. Contact their DES program office in Fort Meade.

items.notable_detail: FBI biometric identity services. Sole-source justification cited urgency of IAFIS modernization and Peraton's unique position as legacy system maintainer.
items.intel_note: Sole-source on Alliant 2 is legally defensible but politically risky in the current climate. DOGE has flagged sole-source justifications over $50M for review. This may get challenged -- not through GAO protest but through DOGE audit. Peraton should expect scrutiny. Competitors (IDEMIA, NEC) should monitor for any break in sole-source justification -- a competitive follow-on would be worth $300M+.

items.notable_detail: Funding for FY26 Q2 operations. Network modernization on schedule. Leidos has successfully migrated 340K users to the new architecture.
items.intel_note: NGEN-R is Leidos's crown jewel and it's performing well. Don't waste capture dollars trying to unseat them on the recompete (2029). Instead, look at the NGEN-R ecosystem: Leidos needs subs for SD-WAN deployment (Cisco/Aruba), SASE implementation, and Navy tactical edge networking. The sub-tier opportunities are $50-100M each and far more winnable than the prime contract.

items.notable_detail: CIO-SP4 ceiling raised to $52B to accommodate surge in health IT task orders. CIO-SP4 is now the largest health IT GWAC by ceiling value, surpassing CIO-SP3's peak.
items.intel_note: If you're on CIO-SP4, you should be staffing a dedicated task order response team. The velocity of TOs is increasing -- NITAAC issued 47 new TOs in February alone (up from 28/month average in FY25). The sweet spot is $5-25M health data analytics and interoperability TOs from HHS, VA, and CDC. Firms NOT on CIO-SP4: look at subcontracting to prime holders or pursue the 8(a) STARS III vehicle as an alternate path to the same agency buyers.

top_agencies_by_spend.trend: Accelerating -- cyber and C2 driving growth. DOGE cuts hitting civilian agencies are being redirected here.
Stable. VA is DOGE-proof due to Congressional protection. Cloud and EHR modernization are the big spenders.
Border tech surge. CBP and ICE procurement up 22% QoQ on surveillance and biometric systems.

trending_naics.insight: Zero-trust mandates driving demand across all agencies. If you have NIST 800-207 implementation experience, highlight it in every proposal.
Catch-all NAICS benefiting from AI/ML advisory work. Agencies are buying 'AI strategy' consulting before committing to platforms.
IC and Space Force R&D spend. This NAICS is where the classified AI work hides. Watch for BAAs from IARPA and AFRL.

action_items.action: Submit Sources Sought response for CYBERCOM NOCDef recompete
action_items.context: The NOCDef restructuring into 3 task areas is the biggest capture opportunity this quarter. Sources Sought responses shape the acquisition strategy. If you don't respond, CYBERCOM won't know you exist when they finalize the RFP. Multiple awards likely -- you don't need to compete for all 3 task areas.

action_items.action: Register for DISA Industry Day (EITSS-III recompete)
action_items.context: DISA has scheduled an Industry Day for April 8 at Fort Meade. Registration closes March 28. This is your last chance to shape the zero-trust requirements before the draft RFP drops. Bring your technical SME, not your BD lead -- DISA wants to talk architecture, not capabilities briefings.

action_items.action: Assess your USAID subcontract exposure and invoke termination-for-convenience protections
action_items.context: If you're subbing on any USAID contract, your prime should have issued a stop-work or T4C flowdown by now. If they haven't, send a written request for direction. Protect your termination settlement rights -- the 60-day clock for filing claims starts when the prime receives the government's T4C notice, not when they tell you about it.

action_items.action: Set SAM.gov alerts for NAICS 541512 and 541519 at DISA and CYBERCOM
action_items.context: Both agencies are in active procurement mode. DISA alone has 6 solicitations expected in the next 90 days. SAM.gov alerts are free and take 2 minutes. There is no excuse for missing a solicitation because you weren't monitoring.

action_items.action: Start CMMC Level 2 assessment if you plan to bid on ANY DoD work after September 2027
action_items.context: C3PAO assessment wait times are now 4-6 months and growing. ManTech's option exercise this week signals CMMC enforcement is accelerating. The cost of assessment ($50-150K) is trivial compared to being locked out of the DoD market. Companies that wait until 2027 will face 12+ month backlogs.

action_items.action: Contact SAIC's DES program office about subcontracting opportunities
action_items.context: The $450M DES ceiling increase means SAIC is absorbing new scope and needs sub-tier support in cloud migration, identity/access management, and endpoint security. DES is becoming the single front door for defense agency IT. Getting on this vehicle as a sub is a strategic imperative.

action_items.action: Build or acquire FinOps capability for your cloud practice
action_items.context: Accenture Federal's VA win was decided in part by their FinOps commitment. This is becoming a standard evaluation factor. At minimum: get 2-3 staff FinOps Certified Practitioners (FOCP). At best: build a FinOps practice with tooling (Apptio, CloudHealth, or Spot by NetApp) and client case studies.

action_items.action: Review your pipeline for civilian agency exposure and rebalance toward DoD/IC
action_items.context: DOGE disruption is creating a 12-18 month civilian agency freeze. If more than 40% of your pipeline is civilian (especially USAID, ED, EPA, OPM), you need to diversify NOW. DoD cyber, AI, and space are growing. VA and DHS are stable. Start building DoD relationships today -- they take 6-12 months to mature.

action_items.action: Identify IC multi-cloud follow-on opportunities as AWS ECH enters final option year
action_items.context: The IC's $742M AWS cloud hosting contract is in its last year. The follow-on will be multi-cloud and multi-award. Cloud SIs should position for the migration and integration services layer ($200M+ opportunity). Start building relationships with the IC CIO's office and attending IC IT enterprise events.

one_to_watch.headline: CJADC2 Tactical Edge AI -- The $3B Program Nobody's Talking About
one_to_watch.description: Buried in the FY27 budget justification documents is a new program element for 'Combined Joint All-Domain Command and Control -- Tactical Edge Processing' (CJADC2-TEP). This is the Joint Staff's answer to the latency problem in JADC2: instead of backhauling all sensor data to cloud for AI processing, they want AI inference running on ruggedized edge devices at the battalion level and below. The program calls for federated learning across classification domains -- models that train on TS/SCI data but deploy inferences at the unclassified tactical edge.
one_to_watch.why_it_matters: This is the next JEDI/JWCC-scale program, but for edge computing instead of cloud. The $1.24B GDIT award this week is the integration layer -- CJADC2-TEP is the compute layer underneath it. First RFI expected Q1 FY27. Estimated ceiling $3-5B over 10 years. The firms that shape this requirement in the next 6 months will own the competitive landscape for the next decade.
one_to_watch.timeline: RFI expected October-December 2026. Draft RFP late FY27. Award FY28.
what_to_do_now: Read the FY27 PB justification book for PE 0604XXX (classified PE -- request through your cleared facility's SAPCO)
Attend the Joint Staff J6 JADC2 Cross-Functional Team industry engagement on May 15 at the Pentagon (registration opens April 1)
If you have edge AI capabilities (NVIDIA Jetson, Qualcomm SNPE, or custom ASIC experience), start building a past performance portfolio with tactical use cases

Identify your teaming strategy early: this will require hardware (ruggedized edge servers), software (ML inference frameworks), and integration (tactical network expertise). No single firm has all three.
Connect with the GDIT JADC2 team -- they will be the prime integrator and will need edge computing subs. Their win this week makes them the center of gravity for this ecosystem.
